ocr.text: The data presented in the table provides valuable insights into the prevalence of suicidal ideation among various demographic
groups, as measured by the Intraclass Correlation Coefficient (ICC) and the associated 95% confidence interval (CI). Suicidal
thoughts, often categorized under the broader topic of mental health, are critical indicators of an individual's psychological
wellbeing and can signal the need for intervention. The reported ICC value of 0.93 suggests a high level of agreement among
the responses gathered, indicating that the data is reliable and robust. Furthermore, the 95% CI, ranging from 0.88 to 0.96,
underscores the precision of this estimate, highlighting the confidence in the observed prevalence rates. This statistical analysis
is essential for understanding the nuances of suicidal ideation across different populations, allowing mental health professionals
to tailor their approaches to prevention and intervention. By examining these metrics, stakeholders can better assess the extent
of the issue and develop targeted strategies aimed at reducing the incidence of suicidal thoughts, ultimately contributing to
improved mental health outcomes in the community. The implications of these findings are significant, warranting further
exploration and action to address this pressing public health concern.
ICC*  95% CI
Reason for living vs. dying  0.88  (0,78, 0.94)
Desire to make active suicide attempt  0.97  (0.94, 0.98)
Passive suicidal desire  0.80  (0.63, 0.89)
Frequency of suicidal ideation  0.92  (0.85, 0.96)
Attitude towards ideation/wish  0.90  (0.82, 0.95)
Control over suicidal action/acting out/delusions/ hallucinations or self-harm  0.86  (0.74, 0.92)
Deterrents to active attempt  0.84  (0.69, 0.91)
Reasons for contemplating attempt  0.97  (0.95, 0.99)
Method: Specificity/planning of contemplated attempt  0.79  (0.59, 0.89)
Expectancy/anticipation by patient of actual attempt  0.90  (0.82, 0.95)
Delusions/hallucinations of self-harm  0.97  (0.95, 0.99)
InterSePT Total Score  0.96  (0.92, 0.98)
